hints.text: If $A$ [implication|implies] $B$ and $A$ also implies $C$, then $A$ implies the [conjunction|conjunction] $B \land C$. You have two arrows leaving the same bow — they'll hit their targets together. Start by assuming the pair of [implication|implications].
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens] on $A \to B$ (step 2) and $A$ (step 4) — first target hit: $B$.
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens] on $A \to C$ (step 3) and $A$ (step 4) — second target hit: $C$.
Bundle $B$ and $C$ with `and-intro`. Both targets in one quiver.
[assumption|Discharge] the original pair. One arrow, two bullseyes.
